what: Correcting for Bias Fields with nu_correct
title: Core Image Processing
text: MRI images generally offer excellent contrast different soft tissue classes. However, intensity inhomogeneities within radio frequency (RF) field can lead variations tissue type intensities across different spatial locations (e.g., intensities might differ top bottom brain). inhomogeneities non-uniformities can cause problems algorithms rely histograms, quantiles, raw intensities (Zhang, Brady, Smith 2001). Therefore, correcting image inhomogeneities critical step many neuroimaging analyses. Freesurfer function nu_correct performs non-uniformity correction using method described (Sled, Zijdenbos, Evans 1998). freesurfer R function name execute correction return processed image. Freesurfer nu_correct command typically requires input MINC format. Now, can pass MINC file directly nu_correct function. function run Freesurfer correction automatically convert resulting MINC output back nifti object use R. can see, output bert_mnc indeed nifti object. can now visualize corrected image alongside estimated bias field (log-transformed better display) observe areas differentially corrected, seen . Ultimately, correction helps make intensities within brain spatially homogeneous. ’s worth noting method different ’s implemented FSL (Jenkinson et al. 2012) (consequently fslr), offering alternative correction method wasn’t previously available R users. can also pass mask brain (’ll cover brain extraction next) apply correction specific areas interest within brain. addition read_mgz read_mgh, also provide read_mnc wrapper function directly reading MINC files ’ve converted NIfTI. useful feature: pass nifti object directly mri_convert function, automatically handle conversion NIfTI input file running Freesurfer conversion.
code: # Save the NIfTI to a temporary MINC file temp_minc_file = tempfile(fileext = ".mnc") bert_mnc <- nii2mnc(bert_nii_rpi$img, outfile = temp_minc_file) bert_nu = nu_correct(bert_mnc) class(bert_nu) bias_field = finite_img(log(bert_nii_rpi$img / bert_nu))  double_ortho(   bert_nu,   bias_field,   col.y = hotmetal(),   mask = bert_nu > 40 )

title: Running `recon-all`
text: want run specific stages recon-pipeline, can use helper functions: - autorecon1(): Motion correction skull stripping (steps 1-5). - autorecon2(): processing (e.g., intensity normalization registration). - autorecon3(): Final stages, including surface reconstruction quality checks.
code: # Run stages individually autorecon1(   infile = "subject_001.nii",   subjid = "subject01",   outdir = "/output_dir",   verbose = TRUE )  autorecon2(   infile = "subject_001.nii",   subjid = "subject01",   outdir = "/output_dir",   verbose = TRUE )  autorecon3(   infile = "subject_001.nii",   subjid = "subject01",   outdir = "/output_dir",   verbose = FALSE  # Disable verbose logging )

NULL (default), retimg = TRUE, temporary file created. Set outfile = file overwrite input file (warning). retimg Logical; TRUE (default), returns output nifti object. FALSE, returns system command result. reorient Logical; TRUE retimg = TRUE, reorients image loading. Passed neurobase::readnii(). Default FALSE. verbose (logical) print diagnostic messages intern Logical; TRUE, captures command output. Passed base::system(). Default FALSE. opts Character. Additional options Freesurfer function. opts_after_outfile Logical; TRUE, places opts outfile command string. Default FALSE. frontopts Character string options prepend input file command. Default "". bin_app Character string specifying FreeSurfer bin directory appendix. Options "bin" (default) "mni/bin". timeout_seconds Numeric; command timeout seconds. specified, requires R.utils package. Default NULL (timeout). validate_inputs Logical; TRUE (default), validates input files exist running command. ... Additional arguments passed base::system().
